notes: Person 4: exercise intelligence library used by routine generator + live coaching.

exercises.name: Air Squat

setup: Stand tall, feet about shoulder-width apart.
Toes slightly turned out, tripod foot: big toe, little toe, heel.
cues: Sit your hips back and down like a chair.
Knees track over your toes (don’t cave in).
Keep your chest proud and ribs stacked over hips.
coaching.tempo: 2 seconds down, brief pause, drive up
coaching.range_of_motion: As low as you can control while keeping heels down and spine neutral.

common_mistakes.name: Knees cave inward
common_mistakes.looks_like: Knees collapse toward each other during the descent or ascent.
common_mistakes.risk: Increases knee stress and reduces glute engagement.
fix_cues: Press knees gently out over toes.
Spread the floor with your feet.

common_mistakes.name: Heels lift
common_mistakes.looks_like: Weight shifts to toes; heels pop up near the bottom.
common_mistakes.risk: Reduces stability and can round the back.
fix_cues: Shift weight back into your heels.
Shorten range and work deeper gradually.

safety_notes: Keep pain-free range. If knees hurt, reduce depth and slow down.
regressions: Box squat to a high surface

gemini.one_sentence: Sit your hips back and down, knees over toes, then stand tall.
gemini.start_prompt: We’re doing air squats. Think: hips back and down, knees track over toes, chest proud. I’ll cue you if anything drifts.
mid_rep_corrections: Knees out a touch—track over your toes.
Keep your heels heavy—sit back a little.
Chest up—ribs stacked, don’t fold forward.
gemini.interruption_safe_phrase: Pause right where you are—reset your stance when you’re ready.

exercises.name: Reverse Lunge

setup: Stand tall. Hands on hips or at sides.
Brace your core gently—tall posture.
cues: Step one foot back and lower straight down.
Front knee stays over mid-foot; back knee points down.
Push the floor away with the front foot to return.
coaching.range_of_motion: Lower until both knees are comfortably bent; keep torso tall.

common_mistakes.name: Front knee collapses inward
common_mistakes.looks_like: Knee dives toward the inside as you lower/stand.
common_mistakes.risk: Knee stress and loss of stability.
fix_cues: Imagine headlights on your knees pointing forward.

common_mistakes.name: Too narrow stance
common_mistakes.looks_like: Feet on a tightrope causing wobble.

safety_notes: Use a smaller step back if you feel knee pressure.
Hold a wall or chair for balance if needed.

gemini.one_sentence: Step back, drop straight down, then drive up through the front foot.
gemini.start_prompt: Reverse lunges: step back on railroad tracks, torso tall, front knee over mid-foot. Smooth and controlled.
mid_rep_corrections: Widen your stance a bit—more stable.
Front knee tracks over toes—don’t let it cave.
gemini.interruption_safe_phrase: Stop and stand tall. We’ll reset your footing.

exercises.name: Push-Up

setup: Hands under shoulders, fingers spread.
Body in a straight line: head, ribs, hips, heels.
cues: Lower with elbows about 30–45° from your body.
Keep ribs down—don’t sag your low back.
Press the floor away and lock in your plank at the top.
coaching.range_of_motion: Chest toward floor while maintaining a rigid plank.

common_mistakes.name: Hips sag
common_mistakes.looks_like: Low back arches; hips drop below shoulders.

common_mistakes.name: Elbows flare
common_mistakes.looks_like: Elbows out wide at 90°.
fix_cues: Aim elbows toward your back pockets.

safety_notes: If wrists hurt, use fists or elevate hands on a bench/counter.

gemini.one_sentence: Strong plank body, lower with control, press the floor away.
gemini.start_prompt: Push-ups: keep a straight line, elbows about 45 degrees, ribs down. If form breaks, we’ll switch to incline.
mid_rep_corrections: Elbows in a bit—protect those shoulders.
Slow down on the way down—control it.
gemini.interruption_safe_phrase: Hold plank or drop knees—then reset.

exercises.name: Front Plank

setup: Elbows under shoulders (or hands under shoulders).
Feet hip-width for stability.
cues: Ribs down—brace like someone’s about to poke your sides.
Squeeze glutes and press forearms into the floor.
Neck long—look slightly ahead of hands.
breathing: Slow, quiet breaths while staying braced.

common_mistakes.name: Piking hips
common_mistakes.looks_like: Butt shoots up high; shoulders drift back.
fix_cues: Bring hips in line with shoulders.

common_mistakes.name: Low back arch
common_mistakes.looks_like: Belly drops; ribs flare.

safety_notes: Stop if you feel pinching in low back—reset and shorten the hold.

gemini.one_sentence: Ribs down, glutes tight, and breathe while holding a straight line.
gemini.start_prompt: Plank: brace, squeeze glutes, keep ribs down. I want steady breathing—no sagging, no piking.
gemini.interruption_safe_phrase: Drop knees, take a breath, and we’ll set up again.

exercises.name: Glute Bridge

setup: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat near hips.
Arms by sides, ribs down.
cues: Press through heels and lift hips.
Stop when hips are in line with ribs and knees—don’t over-arch.
Squeeze glutes at the top for 1 second.
coaching.range_of_motion: Lift to a neutral hip extension without low-back arching.

common_mistakes.name: Overarching low back
common_mistakes.looks_like: Ribs flare and low back takes over at the top.
fix_cues: Tuck pelvis slightly and squeeze glutes.

common_mistakes.name: Pushing through toes
common_mistakes.looks_like: Heels lighten and hamstrings cramp.
fix_cues: Bring feet a bit closer to hips.

safety_notes: If hamstrings cramp, shorten range and emphasize heel pressure.

gemini.one_sentence: Heels heavy, lift hips, squeeze glutes, keep ribs down.
gemini.start_prompt: Glute bridges: press through heels, lift hips to a straight line, squeeze glutes—no low-back arch.
mid_rep_corrections: Squeeze glutes at the top—own it.
gemini.interruption_safe_phrase: Pause at the bottom, reset ribs down, then continue.

exercises.name: Dead Bug

setup: Lie on your back, knees and hips at 90°, arms up.
Press low back gently into the floor.
cues: Reach opposite arm and leg away slowly.
Keep ribs down and low back in contact with floor.
Return and switch sides with control.
coaching.range_of_motion: Only extend as far as you can keep low back down.

common_mistakes.name: Low back lifts
common_mistakes.looks_like: Arch appears as you extend the leg.
common_mistakes.risk: Low-back strain and loss of core control.

safety_notes: Neck relaxed; keep gaze up. Reduce range if you feel back strain.
regressions: Heel taps (one leg at a time)

gemini.one_sentence: Move slow, keep low back down, and breathe out as you extend.
gemini.start_prompt: Dead bug: keep your low back gently pressed down, reach opposite arm/leg slowly. If your back lifts, shorten the range.
gemini.interruption_safe_phrase: Hug knees in for a breath, then reset.

exercises.name: Bird Dog

setup: Hands under shoulders, knees under hips.
Spine neutral, gaze down.
cues: Reach opposite arm and leg long—don’t lift high.
Keep hips square to the floor.
Return slowly and switch.
coaching.range_of_motion: Long reach with minimal torso movement.

common_mistakes.name: Rotating hips
common_mistakes.looks_like: One hip opens up to the side.
fix_cues: Keep both hip bones facing the floor.

safety_notes: If wrists are sensitive, do fists or forearms.
progressions: Add slow elbow-to-knee tap under body

gemini.one_sentence: Reach long, keep hips square, and don’t wobble.
gemini.start_prompt: Bird dog: opposite arm and leg reach long, hips square, spine neutral. Minimal movement—maximum control.
gemini.interruption_safe_phrase: Come back to hands and knees and breathe.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Good Morning (Hip Hinge)

setup: Stand tall, feet hip-width.
Hands on hips or crossed at chest.
cues: Soft knees, push hips back like closing a car door.
Keep spine long and chest slightly proud.
Stand by squeezing glutes—don’t yank with low back.
coaching.range_of_motion: Hinge until you feel hamstrings stretch while keeping a neutral spine.

common_mistakes.name: Rounding back
common_mistakes.looks_like: Upper back slumps; spine curves.

common_mistakes.name: Squatting instead of hinging
common_mistakes.looks_like: Knees bend a lot and hips drop.
fix_cues: Hips go back more than down.

safety_notes: Keep it pain-free. Reduce range if you feel back discomfort.
regressions: Hip hinge to wall (tap hips to wall)

gemini.one_sentence: Soft knees, hips back, long spine, stand by squeezing glutes.
gemini.start_prompt: Hip hinge: slight knee bend, push hips back, keep a long spine. You should feel hamstrings, not low back.
mid_rep_corrections: Squeeze glutes to stand—don’t crank your back.
gemini.interruption_safe_phrase: Stand tall, shake out legs, and reset your hinge.

exercises.name: Mountain Climber

setup: Start in a strong high plank.
Hands under shoulders, feet back.
cues: Drive knees toward chest, alternating.
Keep hips level—don’t bounce your hips.
Press the floor away and keep shoulders stable.
coaching.range_of_motion: Knee drives as far as you can without hips hiking.

common_mistakes.name: Hips bouncing
common_mistakes.looks_like: Butt pops up and down with each rep.
common_mistakes.risk: Less core control; more fatigue on shoulders.
fix_cues: Slow down slightly to stay stable.

safety_notes: If wrists bother you, do forearm climbers or elevate hands.

gemini.one_sentence: Strong plank, hips steady, knees drive in rhythm.
gemini.start_prompt: Mountain climbers: solid plank first. Keep hips steady and shoulders stacked over hands—then pick up the rhythm.
gemini.interruption_safe_phrase: Hold plank and breathe—then continue when stable.

setup: Stand tall, arms by sides.
cues: Soft knees, land quietly.
Arms and legs move together—smooth rhythm.
Stay tall—don’t hunch shoulders.
breathing: Steady breathing; keep a conversational pace if needed.
coaching.range_of_motion: Comfortable range—no need to go max width.

common_mistakes.name: Loud hard landings
common_mistakes.looks_like: Stomping; knees stiff on landing.

gemini.one_sentence: Quiet feet, soft knees, smooth rhythm.
gemini.start_prompt: Jumping jacks: land softly, keep it smooth. If you want low-impact, switch to step jacks—same rhythm.
gemini.interruption_safe_phrase: Switch to step jacks and breathe.

exercises.name: Wall Sit

setup: Back against a wall, feet about 1–2 feet forward.
Slide down until thighs are at a comfortable angle.
cues: Knees track over toes.
Keep full foot contact—heels down.
Stay tall through the torso—don’t fold forward.
coaching.range_of_motion: Hold at a depth you can maintain with good knee tracking.

common_mistakes.name: Knees too far forward
common_mistakes.looks_like: Knees push far past toes; feet too close to wall.

safety_notes: If knees hurt, come higher and shorten hold.

gemini.one_sentence: Feet a bit forward, knees over toes, breathe and hold.
gemini.start_prompt: Wall sit: back flat, feet forward enough that knees feel good, knees track over toes. Breathe—this burns but you’ve got it.
mid_rep_corrections: Walk feet a bit forward—protect knees.
gemini.interruption_safe_phrase: Slide up a few inches and keep breathing.

exercises.name: Side Plank

setup: Elbow under shoulder, legs stacked or staggered.
Top hand on hip or reaching up.
cues: Lift hips into a straight line—don’t let hips sag.
Press forearm into floor and keep shoulder away from ear.
Brace and breathe.
breathing: Slow breaths while holding the line.
coaching.range_of_motion: Static hold with stacked ribs and hips.

common_mistakes.name: Hips sag
common_mistakes.looks_like: Waistline collapses toward the floor.
common_mistakes.risk: Shoulder strain and less oblique work.

safety_notes: If shoulder feels pinchy, switch to side plank from knees.

gemini.one_sentence: Straight line ribs-to-hips, shoulder stable, breathe.
gemini.start_prompt: Side plank: elbow under shoulder, lift hips into one long line. If it’s too spicy, drop your bottom knee.
mid_rep_corrections: Shoulder away from ear—press the floor.
gemini.interruption_safe_phrase: Drop your bottom knee and reset.

exercises.name: Calf Raise

setup: Stand tall, feet hip-width. Hold a wall lightly if needed.
cues: Rise up onto the balls of your feet.
Pause at the top for 1 second.
Lower slowly—don’t drop.

common_mistakes.name: Bouncing
common_mistakes.looks_like: Quick reps with no control at top or bottom.

safety_notes: If Achilles feels irritated, slow down and reduce range.

gemini.one_sentence: Up tall, pause, then lower slowly with control.
gemini.start_prompt: Calf raises: rise up, pause at the top, and lower slowly—no bouncing.
gemini.interruption_safe_phrase: Shake out calves and reset your stance.

exercises.name: Superman Hold

setup: Lie face down, arms long overhead.
Forehead lightly on floor to start.
cues: Lift arms and legs a little—think ‘long’ not ‘high’.
Squeeze glutes gently and keep neck neutral.
Lower with control.
coaching.range_of_motion: Small lift with long spine; no pinching.

common_mistakes.name: Cranking neck
common_mistakes.looks_like: Head lifts high; chin juts.
fix_cues: Imagine length through crown of head.

safety_notes: If low back pinches, reduce lift or swap for bird dog.

gemini.one_sentence: Lift a little, stay long, neck neutral, and breathe.
gemini.start_prompt: Superman: tiny lift, long spine, squeeze glutes gently. If you feel pinching, we’ll switch to bird dog.
mid_rep_corrections: Squeeze glutes gently—don’t crank low back.
gemini.interruption_safe_phrase: Lower down and take a breath.

exercises.name: Step Jack (Low Impact)

setup: Stand tall, arms relaxed.
cues: Step out to the side as arms reach up.
Step back in as arms come down—steady rhythm.
Keep it quiet and smooth.

common_mistakes.name: Rushing
common_mistakes.looks_like: Choppy steps; shoulders tense.

safety_notes: Great option if you need low impact or quieter movement.

gemini.one_sentence: Step out and in with smooth arms—quiet, low impact cardio.
gemini.start_prompt: Step jacks: low impact, smooth rhythm, shoulders relaxed. This is your ‘keep moving’ option anytime.

exercises.name: Chair Squat (Touch-and-Go)

setup: Stand in front of a chair/bench, feet shoulder-width.
Arms forward for balance if you like.
cues: Sit back until you lightly touch the chair.
Don’t fully relax—tap and stand.
Knees track over toes, heels down.
coaching.range_of_motion: To chair touch with stable knees and heels down.

common_mistakes.name: Plopping onto chair
common_mistakes.looks_like: Dropping fast and relaxing on the chair.

safety_notes: Use a stable chair. If needed, use a higher surface.
progressions: Pause at chair hover (1 sec above chair)

gemini.one_sentence: Sit back, tap the chair lightly, and stand—stay in control.
gemini.start_prompt: Chair squats: sit back to a light tap—don’t plop. Stay engaged and drive up through heels.
gemini.interruption_safe_phrase: Stand tall, reset your stance, and continue.
